content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Implemented the pending lifecycle amendments in runtime code. `src/adapters/fs.rs` now treats current-version non-Linux Unix pid files as authoritative by recording a fallback `proc_start_marker` from `ps`, using that marker for liveness checks when `/proc` start ticks are unavailable, and exposing a shared authoritative-record helper consumed by `run` liveness classification. `src/cli/run.rs` no longer reclaims a terminal-snapshot writer owner up front: `run resume` now tries to acquire its own writer lease first and only reclaims the exact observed owner after contention plus stale proof, while stale-running and post-stop recovery paths still reclaim with prior stale evidence. Added regressions in `tests/cli.rs` for the ordinary failed-snapshot lock race and refreshed the stale cleanup-crash regression to require an actually stale lease before reclaim.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Extended pid tracking in `src/adapters/fs.rs` so pid files can carry a non-Linux Unix `proc_start_marker`, added `pid_record_is_authoritative`, and updated liveness checks to accept either Linux start ticks or the fallback marker while still rejecting legacy partial pid records.
2. [Completed] Reworked `src/cli/run.rs` recovery lock acquisition so `run resume` no longer tears down an observed writer owner for ordinary `Failed`/`Paused` snapshots before proving it stale; it now attempts acquisition first, verifies staleness on contention, and only then reclaims the exact observed owner. Stale running-stop/recovery paths pass explicit stale proof and keep their reclaim behavior.
3. [Completed] Updated CLI regressions in `tests/cli.rs` to cover the live competing worktree-owner race on failed resume, and tightened the stale cleanup-crash regression so reclaim only occurs once the stranded lease is actually stale.
4. [Completed] Ran formatting, focused lifecycle regressions, the full `cargo test` suite, and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` to validate the patch and capture the remaining unrelated lint debt.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --check` passed.
- `cargo test pid_record_with_start_marker_is_authoritative` passed.
- `cargo test --test cli run_status_reports_stale_when_pid_record_missing_proc_start_ticks -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test --test cli run_resume_does_not_reclaim_live_worktree_owner_for_failed_snapshot -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test --features test-stub --test cli cli_run_resume_reclaims_writer_lock_for_failed_snapshot_cleanup_crash -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test` passed.
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` failed only on the pre-existing repo-wide lint findings outside this lifecycle amendment.

## Outstanding Risks

- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` still fails on pre-existing repository-wide lint debt outside this patch set, including findings in `src/adapters/fs.rs`, `src/cli/project.rs`, `src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs`, `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs`, and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs`.
- The new non-Linux Unix pid-authority path is implemented with `ps -o lstart/stat` fallback and covered by shared/unit logic, but it was not exercised end-to-end in this Linux validation environment.
